---
title: "4.5 Pairs and Duplicates"
subtitle: "Implementing Array Algorithms"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit4-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Pairs and Duplicates in Array

### Adjacent elements

Here is a pattern to check for duplicates in an array by accessing a pair of elements next to each other, `array[i]` and `array[i + 1]`.

We need to use an indexed `for` loop to get access to the next element.

```java
// check for duplicates next to each other
for (int i = 0; i < values.length - 1; i++)
{
    if (values[i] == values[i + 1])
    {
        return true;
    }
}
return false;
```

## Duplicates Anywhere

### Compare all pairs

If we want to check for duplicates anywhere in the array, we need to use a nested loop to compare all pairs of elements in the array.

```java
// check for duplicates anywhere in the array
for (int i = 0; i < values.length; i++)
{
    for (int j = i + 1; j < values.length; j++)
    {
        if (values[i] == values[j])
        {
            return true;
        }
    }
}
return false;
```

## Coding Exercise

### `activecode:: sumPairs`

Create a method `sumPairs10(nums)` that returns `true` if there are at least two items in the array `nums` that are adjacent (next to each other) and add up to `10`.

Otherwise return `false`.

## sumPairs Starter

```java
public class Pairs
{
   public static boolean sumPairs10(int[] nums)
   {
       // TODO: if two adjacent numbers in nums add up to 10, return true


       return false;
   }

   public static void main(String[] args)
   {

      int[] nums1 = {1, 2, 8};
      System.out.println(sumPairs10(nums1));

      int[] nums2 = {2, 1, 2};
      System.out.println(sumPairs10(nums2));
   }

}
```

## sumPairs Test Targets

### Runestone checks

Expected output from `main`:

```text
true
false
```

Additional method test:

```text
sumPairs10({2, 4, 6, 8}) -> true
```

## Coding Exercise

### `activecode:: noDups`

Create a method `noDups(nums)` that returns `true` if there are no repeated (duplicate) items in the array `nums`.

It should return `false` if it does find a repeated element using nested loops.

## noDups Starter

```java
public class Pairs
{
   public static boolean noDups(int[] nums)
   {
       // TODO: use nested loops to check if any item appears more than once in nums


       return true;
   }

   public static void main(String[] args)
   {

      int[] nums1 = {1, 2, 8};
      System.out.println(noDups(nums1));

      int[] nums2 = {3, 3, 3};
      System.out.println(noDups(nums2));
   }
}
```

## noDups Test Targets

### Runestone checks

Expected output from `main`:

```text
true
false
```

Additional method test:

```text
noDups({2, 4, 6, 2}) -> false
```

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should include

::: {.tight-list}
- use `i < values.length - 1` when checking adjacent pairs
- access adjacent elements as `values[i]` and `values[i + 1]`
- use nested loops to compare all pairs in an array
- start the inner duplicate-check loop at `i + 1`
- return `true` from `sumPairs10` only when adjacent values add to `10`
- return `false` from `noDups` as soon as a duplicate pair is found
:::

## End

### 4.5 Part 3 complete

Next: rotate and reverse.
